chunks.text: Course: EE 228A | EECS at UC Berkeley EE 228A. High Speed Communications Networks Catalog Description: Descriptions, models, and approaches to the design and management of networks. Optical transmission and switching technologies are described and analyzed using deterministic, stochastic, and simulation models. FDDI, DQDB, SMDS, Frame Relay, ATM, networks, and SONET. Applications demanding high-speed communication. Units: 3 Also Offered As: ELENG 228A Related Areas: Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS) Prerequisites: EL ENG 122; and EL ENG 226A (may be taken concurrently). Formats: Spring: 3 hours of lecture per week Fall: 3 hours of lecture per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: No Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
